methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $56M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Formic, a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Saman Farid and Misa Ilkhechi, has raised $56M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 9 private robotics companies and 1 public comparable, spanning stages from Series A through Series B with valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression, using the $56M total raised as a proxy base and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implies a valuation of $112M. Peer-median analysis across 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—including Foundation Robotics ($3.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), and Collaborative Robotics ($500M)—yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with observed range of $300M to $10.0B.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $95M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $881M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in comparable-company selection and stage-appropriate discounting. The peer-median method reflects the sector's current capital intensity and investor appetite, though the substantial gap between stage-progression and peer medians highlights sensitivity to assumptions about Formic's relative maturity, technology differentiation, and capital efficiency versus the identified peer set.
